---
title: "Whale Hunters"
jupyter: python3
---

::: {.hero-banner}
## Whale Hunters: A Volatility Breakout Strategy

This project studies a volatility and channel breakout strategy for liquid ETFs. It is organized as a Quarto website backed by an `analysis/` Python package, a `shinybroker` data pipeline, and a supervised-learning extension that uses implied-volatility features from Interactive Brokers.
:::

```{python}
from analysis.report_data import load_baseline_report, load_holdout_report
from IPython.display import HTML

analysis = load_baseline_report()
live_holdout = load_holdout_report()
overview = analysis["overview"].loc[analysis["overview"]["Field"] != "Project"].reset_index(drop=True)
metrics = analysis["metrics_display"]
holdout_comparison = live_holdout["comparison"].copy()
holdout_comparison["strategy"] = holdout_comparison["strategy"].replace(
    {
        "baseline_live_holdout": "Baseline",
        "ml_hard_filter_live_holdout": "ML hard filter",
        "ml_position_scaling_live_holdout": "ML position scaling",
    }
)
holdout_equity = live_holdout["equity_curves"].copy()
holdout_equity["strategy"] = holdout_equity["strategy"].replace(
    {
        "baseline_live_holdout": "Baseline",
        "ml_hard_filter_live_holdout": "ML hard filter",
        "ml_position_scaling_live_holdout": "ML position scaling",
    }
)
baseline_holdout = holdout_comparison.loc[holdout_comparison["strategy"] == "Baseline"].iloc[0]
scaling_holdout = holdout_comparison.loc[holdout_comparison["strategy"] == "ML position scaling"].iloc[0]
hard_filter_holdout = holdout_comparison.loc[holdout_comparison["strategy"] == "ML hard filter"].iloc[0]
holdout_trades = live_holdout["scaled_trades"].copy()
holdout_prices = holdout_equity.loc[holdout_equity["strategy"] == "Baseline", ["date", "close"]].copy()
```

## Results Snapshot

The main validation test is a fixed live holdout from `{python} live_holdout["summary"]["holdout_start"]` through `{python} live_holdout["summary"]["holdout_end"]`. The model is trained only on labeled breakout events before October 2025, then applied to the later period as unseen data.

```{python}
cards = [
    ("Holdout Window", f'{live_holdout["summary"]["holdout_start"]} to {live_holdout["summary"]["holdout_end"]}', "Model frozen before October 2025"),
    ("Baseline Return", f'{baseline_holdout["total_return"]:.2%}', f'{int(baseline_holdout["trade_count"])} trades'),
    ("ML Scaling Return", f'{scaling_holdout["total_return"]:.2%}', f'Sharpe {scaling_holdout["sharpe_ratio"]:.2f}'),
    ("Hard Filter", f'{int(hard_filter_holdout["trade_count"])} trade', "Too sparse for final use"),
]

HTML(
    '<div class="snapshot-grid">'
    + ''.join(
        f'''
        <div class="snapshot-card">
          <div class="snapshot-label">{label}</div>
          <div class="snapshot-value">{value}</div>
          <div class="snapshot-note">{note}</div>
        </div>
        '''
        for label, value, note in cards
    )
    + '</div>'
)
```

The out-of-sample result supports the final design choice: the ML model adds value as a **position-sizing overlay**, while the hard filter is too aggressive because it leaves too few trades to rely on.

```{python}
import plotly.graph_objects as go

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=holdout_prices["date"],
        y=holdout_prices["close"],
        mode="lines",
        name="QQQ close",
        line=dict(color="#2563eb", width=2.4),
        hovertemplate="%{x|%Y-%m-%d}<br>Close: $%{y:.2f}<extra></extra>",
    )
)

if not holdout_trades.empty:
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=holdout_trades["entry_timestamp"],
            y=holdout_trades["entry_price"],
            mode="markers",
            name="Entry",
            marker=dict(symbol="triangle-up", color="#059669", size=13, line=dict(color="#ffffff", width=1)),
            customdata=holdout_trades[["size_multiplier", "breakout_strength", "qty"]],
            hovertemplate=(
                "%{x|%Y-%m-%d}<br>Entry: $%{y:.2f}"
                "<br>ML size multiplier: %{customdata[0]:.2f}"
                "<br>Breakout strength: %{customdata[1]:.2f}"
                "<br>Qty: %{customdata[2]:.1f}<extra></extra>"
            ),
        )
    )
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=holdout_trades["exit_timestamp"],
            y=holdout_trades["exit_price"],
            mode="markers",
            name="Exit",
            marker=dict(symbol="triangle-down", color="#d97706", size=13, line=dict(color="#ffffff", width=1)),
            customdata=holdout_trades[["exit_reason", "trade_return", "pnl"]],
            hovertemplate=(
                "%{x|%Y-%m-%d}<br>Exit: $%{y:.2f}"
                "<br>Reason: %{customdata[0]}"
                "<br>Trade return: %{customdata[1]:.2%}"
                "<br>PnL: $%{customdata[2]:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
            ),
        )
    )

fig.update_layout(
    title="Out-of-Sample Breakout Entries And Exits: October 2025-April 2026",
    height=430,
    hovermode="x unified",
    yaxis_title="QQQ Price",
    legend_title_text="",
)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
holdout_comparison
```

## Strategy Snapshot

```{python}
overview
```

## Project Scope

- Build a transparent breakout strategy with explicit entry and exit rules.
- Backtest the strategy on historical price data using a repeatable Python workflow.
- Log every trade so entries, exits, and holding periods are auditable.
- Extend the baseline strategy with supervised learning tests for breakout quality, including a hard filter and the final position-scaling overlay.

The final baseline is a tuned `QQQ` channel-breakout strategy with explicit stop-loss, trailing-stop, timeout, and breakout-failure exits. On top of that baseline, the project adds a compact `QQQ`-only logistic model that uses breakout quality plus options-implied-volatility features. The report compares a hard model filter with a position-scaling overlay and uses the overlay as the final version because it keeps the breakout opportunity set while changing capital allocation.

## What To Review

- [Results](results.qmd) leads with the fixed October 2025-April 2026 holdout evaluation and performance graph.
- [Asset Selection](asset-selection.qmd) explains the ETF universe and why `QQQ` became the showcase asset.
- [Methodology](methodology.qmd) documents the final trading rules, labels, feature set, and parameter choices.
- [Trade Log](trade-log.qmd) contains the blotter, ledger preview, and downloadable output files.
- [Monitoring](monitoring.qmd) defines how to judge whether the strategy is still behaving as expected after deployment.
- [About](about.qmd) introduces the project team.
